title: Building a dashboard in monday.com to track key ecommerce KPIs (AOV, conversion rate, returns)
excerpt: I’m looking for guidance from users who have experience building ecommerce analytics dashboards inside monday.com. My goal is to create a fully functional KPI dashboard that tracks critical ecommerce metrics like Average…

posts.author: TarunNagar (Tarun Nagar)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’m looking for guidance from users who have experience building ecommerce analytics dashboards inside monday.com. My goal is to create a fully functional KPI dashboard that tracks critical ecommerce metrics like Average Order Value (AOV), conversion rate, return rate, revenue trends, and customer behavior insights — all within monday.com’s board or dashboard environment.

I’m hoping to learn from people who’ve already built similar dashboards or connected ecommerce stores (Shopify, WooCommerce, Magento, BigCommerce, etc.) to monday.com. Specifically, I’d like insights into:

  • Best ways to structure boards or workflows for accurate ecommerce tracking

    Integrations or automation tools you used (Shopify integration, Zapier, Make, custom API scripts, monday apps)

  • How to automatically pull order, traffic, and return data into monday.com

  • Recommended widgets or views for visualizing KPIs like AOV, CR, or revenue

  • How to handle real-time or near real-time data updates

  • Any limitations you faced with monday.com dashboards vs. dedicated BI tools

  • Tips for blending multiple data sources into a single dashboard

  • Best practices for reporting and sharing ecommerce insights with teams

I’m especially interested in hearing about real-world setups, not just theoretical ideas—screenshots, workflows, and examples of how you structured your data would be incredibly helpful.
